raw_consumer_statement:
I suspected fraudulent practice with Hotelbookings.com ( expedia ). I called Citibank within XXXX minutes, I followed their advice, subsequently I was billed for a transaction where no services or product was exchanged. Citibank reversed it's decision and favored the merchant. 
This a copy of my last letter to Citibank XX/XX/year> Dispute claim : D XXXX Hotelbookings claim of no valid cancellation, their was no reservation to be canceled.. 
When I suspected a fraudulent practice, I called Citibank card services and explained my concern. Citibank informed me that no transaction went to the XXXX XXXX and that I should make my own reservation. 
I verified by calling the resort myself and was told no reservation were made. 
{ can be verified by calling the XXXX @ XXXX I spoke to XXXX and XXXX } Now Citibank is reversing their decision and I am being penalized for following Citibanks advice, I would hope I do not have to contact the bureau of consumer financial protection. 
* Remember no services or goods were rendered by the merchant * The alleged transaction was with a unauthorized person * Hotel bookings claimed that their was no way to correct the dates * no e-mail was ever sent to my address ( XXXX )
